text: Dynamic warm-ups are preferred over static stretching before athletic performance due to increased potential to improve performance and reduce injury.
Dynamic warm-ups enhance readiness by activating musculoskeletal, neurologic, cardiovascular, and psychological systems before performance.
Pre-activity static stretching alone is inferior to dynamic warm-ups for both performance enhancement and injury reduction.
Effective dynamic warm-ups should be multifaceted and sport-specific rather than generic.
A combination of pre-performance activities (not solely stretching) represents evolving best practice for warm-up protocols.
